patterns.title: Measure word 个 (gè) — general
patterns.explanation: 个 (gè) is the most common and versatile measure word, used for people and many objects when no specific measure word applies.
patterns.template: 我有一___ {[朋友|学生|苹果|老师|人]}。
patterns.hint: 个 (gè) is used with people and general objects.

patterns.title: Measure word 只 (zhī) — animals
patterns.explanation: 只 (zhī) is used for small animals like cats, dogs, birds, and also for some body parts in pairs.
patterns.hint: 只 (zhī) is the measure word for small animals.

patterns.title: Measure word 本 (běn) — books
patterns.explanation: 本 (běn) is used for books, notebooks, and bound publications.
patterns.template: 我买了一___ {[书|字典|杂志|本子]}。
patterns.hint: 本 (běn) is the measure word for books and notebooks.

patterns.title: 是 (shì) sentences — X is Y
patterns.explanation: 是 (shì) means 'is/am/are'. Basic structure: Subject + 是 + Noun. Unlike English, no 'a/an/the' is needed.
patterns.template: 我 ___ {[学生|老师|医生|他的朋友]}。
patterns.hint: 是 connects the subject to a noun or identity.

patterns.title: 有 (yǒu) — to have / there is
patterns.explanation: 有 means both 'to have' (possession) and 'there is/are' (existence). Negation: 没有 (méiyǒu).
patterns.hint: 有 expresses possession. Its negation is 没有, never 不有.

patterns.title: Basic sentence order — Subject-Verb-Object
patterns.explanation: Chinese follows Subject-Verb-Object (SVO) order, similar to English. Time and place words usually come before the verb.

patterns.title: Time before verb
patterns.explanation: Time expressions come before the verb in Chinese, not after as in English.

patterns.title: Aspect particle 了 (le) — completed action
patterns.explanation: 了 after a verb marks a completed action. It does not mean past tense — it marks completion. 我吃了 = I ate / I have eaten.
patterns.template: 我昨天吃___ {[苹果|饭|很多东西|一个苹果]}。
patterns.hint: 了 after the verb marks the action as completed.

patterns.title: Aspect particle 过 (guò) — past experience
patterns.explanation: 过 marks that something has been experienced at some point in the past. 我去过北京 = I have been to Beijing (at some point).
patterns.template: 我 {[去过北京|吃过北京烤鸭|看过这本书|学过日语]}。
patterns.hint: 过 marks a past experience, not just completion.

patterns.title: 的/地/得 — structural particles
patterns.explanation: 的 links modifier to noun (红色的苹果). 地 links adverb to verb (慢慢地走). 得 links verb to result/complement (跑得很快).
patterns.template: 她跑___ {[很快|很慢|不快|很好]}。
patterns.hint: 得 connects a verb to its degree complement.

patterns.title: 的 — modifying nouns
patterns.explanation: 的 links an adjective or possessive to the noun that follows. 我的书 (my book), 漂亮的花 (beautiful flower).
patterns.template: {[我|他|漂亮|聪明]} ___ 朋友很好。
patterns.hint: 的 connects a modifier (adjective or possessive) to the following noun.

patterns.title: 不 vs 没 — negation
patterns.explanation: 不 (bù) negates habitual actions, states, and future intent. 没 (méi) negates completed actions and 有. Never use 不 with 有.

sentences.error: 有 is always negated with 没, never 不。
习惯性的喜好用不，不用没。

patterns.title: Measure word 杯 (bēi) — cups/glasses
patterns.explanation: 杯 is used for cups, glasses, and beverages served in a cup or glass.
patterns.template: 我想喝一___ {[水|茶|咖啡|果汁]}。
patterns.hint: 杯 is the measure word for drinks in a cup or glass.

patterns.title: Aspect particle 着 (zhe) — ongoing state
patterns.explanation: 着 after a verb indicates a continuing state or action as background. 他站着 (he is standing). Often used with another verb: 站着说话。
patterns.template: 他 {[站着|坐着|穿着外套|拿着书]} 说话。
patterns.hint: 着 indicates a sustained state that accompanies another action.

patterns.title: 把 (bǎ) construction — disposal
patterns.explanation: 把 moves the object before the verb to emphasize what is done to it. Structure: Subject + 把 + Object + Verb + Complement. The verb cannot stand alone after 把.

sentences.error: After 把, the verb needs a complement — 放 alone is insufficient.
Perception verbs like 看见 don't work with 把。

patterns.title: Resultative complements
patterns.explanation: A resultative complement directly follows the verb to show the result of the action. 写完 (finish writing), 听懂 (understand by listening), 吃完 (finish eating).
patterns.template: 我已经把作业做___ {[完|好|完了|好了]}。
patterns.hint: 完 means 'finished', 好 means 'done properly' — both are valid resultative complements.

patterns.title: Topic-comment structure
patterns.explanation: Chinese frequently places the topic first, then comments on it. The topic is what the sentence is about; the comment says something about it.

answers.note: SVO order without topic fronting is also correct.

patterns.title: Comparison with 比 (bǐ)
patterns.explanation: Structure: A + 比 + B + Adjective. The adjective comes at the end. Do not add 更 (gèng) after 比 in standard comparisons.

sentences.error: 更 is redundant after 比 — the comparison is already expressed by 比.
很 cannot be used with 比 comparisons. Use 高多了 or 高一点儿 instead.

patterns.title: Sentence-final 吧 (ba) — suggestion or assumption
patterns.explanation: 吧 softens statements into assumptions (I assume...) or suggestions (let's...). It seeks confirmation or agreement.
patterns.hint: 吧 expresses an assumption seeking confirmation — like 'right?' or 'I suppose'.

patterns.title: Potential complements — 得/不 between verb and result
patterns.explanation: Insert 得 (possibility) or 不 (impossibility) between verb and complement. 做得完 (can finish), 做不完 (can't finish).
patterns.template: 这本书太难了，我看___ {[不懂|得懂|不完|得完]}。
patterns.hint: 不 inserted between verb and complement expresses impossibility.

patterns.title: 被 (bèi) — passive construction
patterns.explanation: 被 introduces the agent in a passive sentence, often with a negative or undesirable outcome. Structure: Subject + 被 + Agent + Verb + Complement.

sentences.error: 被 is typically used for undesirable or uncontrolled events — positive emotions like 爱 sound unnatural.

patterns.title: 尽管…还是 — concessive clause
patterns.explanation: 尽管 (jǐnguǎn) means 'although/even though', paired with 还是 or 仍然 in the main clause for contrast.
patterns.template: 尽管很累，他___ {[还是坚持工作|仍然继续学习|还是去了|仍然没有放弃]}。
patterns.hint: 尽管 introduces a concession; 还是/仍然 in the main clause means 'still/nevertheless'.

patterns.title: 既然…就 — since/given that
patterns.explanation: 既然 (jìrán) means 'since' or 'given that' — a logical conclusion follows. Structure: 既然 + reason, Subject + 就 + conclusion.
patterns.template: 既然你已经决定了，___ 去做吧。
patterns.hint: 就 in the main clause introduces the logical consequence.
